name: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal USA
mission: TO GENERATE CHARITABLE DONATIONS FOR HELPING IN THE TREATMENT OF CANCER IN THIRD WORLD COUNTRIES AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF THE SHAUKAT KHANUM MEMORIAL CANCER HOSPITAL AND RESEARCH CENTER. SPECIFIC GOALS INCLUDE (1) TO INITIATE CHARITABLE FUND RAISING ACTIVITIES, (2) TO RECRUIT PROFESSIONAL AND NON- PROFESSIONAL STAFF AND EXPENSES FOR THE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE, (3) TO SUPPLY FINANCIAL SUPPORT FOR THE PAYMENT OF MEDICINES, DIAGNOSTIC AND THERAPEUTIC EQUIPMENT FOR THE SHAUKAT KHANUM MEMORIAL CANCER HOSPITAL AND RESEARCH CENTER. (4) TO ASSIST IN THE RECRUITMENT OF ALL MEDICAL AND ANCILLARY PERSONNEL FOR THE SHAUKAT KHANUM MEMORIAL CANCER HOSPITAL AND RESEARCH CENTER.
programs: Cancer Registry and Clinical Data Management
scoreSummary: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal, Inc. shows strong alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (major risk deductions applied).

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is high due to the organization's strong fit for donors seeking to support global health and urgent medical needs [1]. The charity is explicitly Zakat-eligible, providing clear pathways for donors to fulfill religious obligations through specialized cancer care programs [2][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization maintains a high Charity Navigator score of 95.0/100 and a program expense ratio of 97.4%, indicating that nearly all revenue of $23,259,494 is directed toward clinical and diagnostic services [1][3]. While it excels in financial health and working capital, the impact score reflects a focus on capacity building where specific cost-per-beneficiary data is not currently available [1].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize maximum financial efficiency and want their Zakat to directly fund cancer treatment for the poor in Pakistan.
donor_motivations: Want 97.4% of their donation to reach program services
Prioritize high-urgency health interventions like oncology
Seeking to support established Pakistani healthcare institutions
giving_considerations: Donations support a highly centralized operation in Pakistan
Governance is lean, which may not suit donors preferring large, diverse boards
Focus is on direct service rather than systemic advocacy
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking a charity with a large, multi-member US-based governing board or those looking for diverse geographic impact outside of Pakistan.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: While the charity tracks <cite id="14">thousands of treated cases</cite>, it lacks an <cite id="18">explicit causal pathway document</cite> or independent RCT-level evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: IKCA operates on the premise that providing free or subsidized cancer diagnostics and treatment to the poor reduces mortality and prevents families from falling into deeper poverty due to medical costs.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the high volume of clinical services translates into long-term survivability for patients.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="4">26.2% revenue growth rate</cite> and expanding clinical needs in Pakistan, the organization can absorb significant additional capital.

peer_comparison.differentiator: IKCA's <cite id="1">97.4% program ratio</cite> is significantly higher than the <cite id="9">peer median of 88.2%</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Broader geographic reach across multiple crisis zones.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">97.4% program expense ratio</cite>, significantly exceeding the <cite id="9">peer program ratio median of 88.2%</cite>.
IKCA demonstrates elite fundraising efficiency, spending only <cite id="10">$0.02 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions.
The charity holds a <cite id="3">95/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, including a <cite id="11">perfect 100 score for Leadership</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: IKCA's financial resilience is a primary differentiator. With <cite id="8">9.8 months of working capital</cite>, the organization is well-positioned to sustain its <cite id="12">diagnostic and pharmacy programs</cite> even during economic fluctuations. Its growth trajectory is equally impressive, moving from <cite id="13">$11.2 million in 2021 to nearly $18 million in 2023</cite>. This expansion supports a wide array of services, including the treatment of <cite id="14">6,446 malignant neoplasms</cite> and specialized care for <cite id="15">518 pediatric cancer cases</cite> as documented in recent registry reports.
rich_narrative.summary: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal, Inc. (IKCA) serves as a critical financial lifeline for cancer patients in Pakistan, primarily supporting the Shaukat Khanum Memorial Cancer Hospital and Research Centre. The organization demonstrates an exceptional commitment to financial efficiency, reporting a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 97.4%</cite> in its most recent filings. This ensures that nearly every dollar donated is directed toward <cite id="2">clinical and diagnostic services</cite> for those who cannot afford specialized oncology care. IKCA has achieved a <cite id="3">95/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting high standards of accountability and leadership. Over the last three years, the charity has shown significant growth, with a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 26.2%</cite>, reaching <cite id="5">$17,974,012 in total revenue</cite> by 2023. As a <cite id="6">zakat-eligible entity</cite>, it provides a transparent mechanism for Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations, specifically targeting the <cite id="7">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> asnaf categories. While the organization excels in financial health, maintaining <cite id="8">9.8 months of working capital</cite>, its governance structure is notably lean, which presents both high agility and potential oversight risks.
